title: Receipt
description: The cryptographic receipt is the final, immutable record of a completed action in the RIO Receipt Protocol. It binds together the action request, AI recommendation, human approval, and execution outcome into a single signed, hash-chained artifact. A valid receipt proves that the full chain was followed and that the outcome is non-repudiable.

request_id.description: The request_id of the ActionRequest this receipt covers.

recommendation_id.description: The recommendation_id of the AIRecommendation that was produced for this request.

approval_id.description: The approval_id of the ApprovalRecord that authorized execution.

execution_id.description: The execution_id of the ExecutionRecord that records what was done.

action_type.description: The action_type from the original ActionRequest. Copied here for fast audit lookup without requiring a join.

requested_by.description: The requested_by value from the original ActionRequest.

executed_by.description: The executed_by value from the ExecutionRecord.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this receipt was generated.

ledger_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON of this receipt (excluding the ledger_hash and signature fields themselves). Used to verify receipt integrity.

previous_hash.description: The ledger_hash of the immediately preceding receipt in the audit log. For the first receipt in the ledger, this value must be the string 'GENESIS'. This field creates the tamper-evident hash chain.

chain_index.description: The zero-based position of this receipt in the ledger chain. The first receipt has chain_index 0.

signature.description: Cryptographic signature over the canonical form of this receipt (including ledger_hash, excluding signature). Produced by the receipt-issuing system's private key.

verification_method.description: The cryptographic method used to produce and verify the signature field.

status.description: The current status of this receipt. A receipt is valid when first issued. It may be marked invalidated if a compliance review determines it was produced in error.
